title: Minimal source-change re-fetch check (hash compare) — manual; automated in M2
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual derives definitions from allow-listed sources whose license terms and content can change over time as oncology terminology and standards evolve. A minimal source-change watcher is required in M0 to detect when a source's hash differs from its stored snapshot, triggering re-verification before further definitions are drafted from that source. A fully automated watcher is planned for M2 (watcher-001).
objective: Implement a minimal script that re-fetches each allow-listed source URL, computes a SHA-256 hash of the retrieved content, and compares it to the stored snapshotHash in the allow-list YAML, reporting any drift as a warning for manual re-verification.
acceptanceCriteria: A script (tooling/check-source-hashes.js or similar) is added that: reads sources/term-source-allow-list.yaml, re-fetches each non-restricted source URL, computes SHA-256 of the retrieved content, and compares it to the stored snapshotHash
Any hash mismatch is reported as a WARNING with the source name, URL, stored hash, and new hash — not silently ignored
The script is documented in a brief README or inline comment explaining how to run it manually
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass on the PR
The script does not automatically update the allow-list; drift is flagged for human re-verification only
output: PR adding tooling/check-source-hashes.js (or equivalent) with documentation for manual use
